content: Laser cleaning technology is revolutionizing surface preparation and contaminant removal across industries like Automotive, Aerospace, Manufacturing, Shipbuilding, and Historical Restoration, particularly for iron and steel components. Utilizing high-intensity pulsed laser beams, this method ablates rust, oxides, and coatings through photothermal and photochemical interactions, leaving the substrate intact without mechanical abrasion. In Automotive, laser cleaning strips rust from chassis frames before painting, ensuring superior adhesion. Aerospace benefits from precise removal of iron oxide layers on turbine parts, maintaining strict tolerances. Manufacturing employs it for cleaning molds and dies, where even minor residue can impact product quality. Shipbuilding uses lasers to de-rust hulls, enhancing weld integrity during repairs. In Historical Restoration, delicate iron artifacts—like 18th-century wrought iron gates—are restored without damaging patina, using low-energy settings (e.g., 50-100 mJ/pulse). While not flawless—surface micro-pitting can occur if parameters aren’t optimized—laser cleaning offers unmatched precision and eco-friendliness over traditional sandblasting or chemical methods.
